steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The sentence opens with a dependent clause: "Because regolith is both readily available and high in oxygen ______." Everything after the blank is the independent clause: "scientists have wondered whether it could be used as a potential source of oxygen for future lunar settlements." A dependent clause introducing a sentence must be separated from the main clause by a comma.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'content and': adds the conjunction "and," which connects the dependent clause to the main clause as if both were independent, creating an ungrammatical sentence with no main clause. ✗
  • Choice B — 'content,': places a comma after the dependent clause, correctly marking the boundary before the main clause. ✓
  • Choice C — 'content': omits any punctuation, fusing the dependent clause into the main clause without the required boundary marker. ✗
  • Choice D — 'content, and': adds both a comma and "and," which—like Choice A—produces an ungrammatical sentence lacking a proper main clause. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
